evidence_quote: The battle took place at the chief crossing of the Trident, near the crossroads of the river road and the kingsroad
asserted_relation: Battle of the Trident took place at the Ruby Ford (chief crossing of the Trident)

evidence_quote: They had come together at the ford of the Trident while the battle crashed around them, Robert with his warhammer and his great antlered helm, the Targaryen prince armored all in black. On his breastplate was the three-headed dragon of his House, wrought all in rubies that flashed like fire in the sunlight. The waters of the Trident ran red around the hooves of their destriers as they circled and clashed, again and again, until at last a crushing blow from Robert's hammer stove in the dragon and the chest beneath it. When Ned had finally come on the scene, Rhaegar lay dead in the stream, while men of both armies scrabbled in the swirling waters for rubies knocked free of his armor.
asserted_relation: Robert Baratheon killed Rhaegar Targaryen with a warhammer blow at the Trident — the decisive act of the battle

evidence_quote: They had come together at the ford of the Trident while the battle crashed around them, Robert with his warhammer and his great antlered helm, the Targaryen prince armored all in black. On his breastplate was the three-headed dragon of his House, wrought all in rubies that flashed like fire in the sunlight. The waters of the Trident ran red around the hooves of their destriers as they circled and clashed, again and again, until at last a crushing blow from Robert's hammer stove in the dragon and the chest beneath it. When Ned had finally come on the scene, Rhaegar lay dead in the stream, while men of both armies scrabbled in the swirling waters for rubies knocked free of his armor.
asserted_relation: Rhaegar Targaryen was killed by Robert Baratheon at the Trident — died in the ford, rubies scattered in the stream

evidence_quote: On the Trident, Ser Barristan here cut down a dozen good men, Robert's friends and mine. When they brought him to us, grievously wounded and near death, Roose Bolton urged us to cut his throat, but your brother said, 'I will not kill a man for loyalty, nor for fighting well,' and sent his own maester to tend Ser Barristan's wounds.
asserted_relation: Barristan Selmy fought for the royalists at the Trident, cut down a dozen rebel fighters, and was gravely wounded

evidence_quote: On the Trident, when their father fell wounded, it was Lyn who snatched up Lady Forlorn and slew the man who'd cut him down. Whilst Lyonel was carrying the old man back to the maesters in the rear, Lyn led his charge against the Dornishmen threatening Robert's left, broke their lines to pieces, and slew Lewyn Martell.
asserted_relation: Lewyn Martell commanded the Dornish flank for the royalists at the Trident and was killed by Lyn Corbray

evidence_quote: On the Trident, when their father fell wounded, it was Lyn who snatched up Lady Forlorn and slew the man who'd cut him down. Whilst Lyonel was carrying the old man back to the maesters in the rear, Lyn led his charge against the Dornishmen threatening Robert's left, broke their lines to pieces, and slew Lewyn Martell.
asserted_relation: Lyn Corbray fought for the rebels at the Trident, led the charge that broke the Dornish flank, and killed Lewyn Martell with Lady Forlorn

evidence_quote: Septa Mordane pointed out Lord Jason Mallister, in indigo chased with silver, the wings of an eagle on his helm. He had cut down three of Rhaegar's bannermen on the Trident.
asserted_relation: Jason Mallister fought for the rebels at the Trident and slew three of Rhaegar's bannermen

evidence_quote: On the Trident, Ser Barristan here cut down a dozen good men, Robert's friends and mine.
asserted_relation: Eddard Stark was present and commanded rebel forces at the Trident; quote is Ned recalling it in first person — 'Robert's friends and mine'

evidence_quote: Robert announced his claim for the Iron Throne and led the combined Arryn, Baratheon, Stark, and Tully host of rebels.
asserted_relation: Hoster Tully's forces were part of the combined rebel host at the Trident

evidence_quote: Ser Barristan Selmy and Ser Jonothor Darry, also of the Kingsguard, were sent to rally the scattered forces of Lord Connington.
asserted_relation: Jonothor Darry served as Kingsguard with the royalist army at the Trident (rallied forces for Rhaegar pre-battle)

evidence_quote: Lord Wyman Manderly granted the Wolf's Den to Ser Bartimus as a reward for saving his life during the battle.
asserted_relation: Wyman Manderly fought at the Trident (rebel side); his life was saved by Bartimus during the battle

evidence_quote: They waited before the round tower, the red mountains of Dorne at their backs, their white cloaks blowing in the wind.
asserted_relation: The combat took place at the tower of joy in the Red Mountains of Dorne

evidence_quote: They had been seven against three, yet only two had lived to ride away; Eddard Stark himself and the little crannogman, Howland Reed.
asserted_relation: Eddard Stark fought at the combat at the tower of joy and was one of only two survivors

evidence_quote: They had been seven against three, yet only two had lived to ride away; Eddard Stark himself and the little crannogman, Howland Reed.
asserted_relation: Howland Reed fought at the combat at the tower of joy and was one of only two survivors

evidence_quote: Martyn Cassel had perished with the rest. Ned had pulled the tower down afterward, and used its bloody stones to build eight cairns upon the ridge.
asserted_relation: Martyn Cassel fought and died at the combat at the tower of joy

evidence_quote: In the dream his friends rode with him, as they had in life. Proud Martyn Cassel, Jory's father; faithful Theo Wull; Ethan Glover, who had been Brandon's squire; Ser Mark Ryswell, soft of speech and gentle of heart; the crannogman, Howland Reed; Lord Dustin on his great red stallion.
asserted_relation: Theo Wull rode with Ned to the tower of joy (dream narration = second-hand recall)

evidence_quote: In the dream his friends rode with him, as they had in life. Proud Martyn Cassel, Jory's father; faithful Theo Wull; Ethan Glover, who had been Brandon's squire; Ser Mark Ryswell, soft of speech and gentle of heart; the crannogman, Howland Reed; Lord Dustin on his great red stallion.
asserted_relation: Ethan Glover rode with Ned to the tower of joy (dream narration = second-hand recall)

evidence_quote: In the dream his friends rode with him, as they had in life. Proud Martyn Cassel, Jory's father; faithful Theo Wull; Ethan Glover, who had been Brandon's squire; Ser Mark Ryswell, soft of speech and gentle of heart; the crannogman, Howland Reed; Lord Dustin on his great red stallion.
asserted_relation: Ser Mark Ryswell rode with Ned to the tower of joy (dream narration = second-hand recall)

evidence_quote: In the dream his friends rode with him, as they had in life. Proud Martyn Cassel, Jory's father; faithful Theo Wull; Ethan Glover, who had been Brandon's squire; Ser Mark Ryswell, soft of speech and gentle of heart; the crannogman, Howland Reed; Lord Dustin on his great red stallion.
asserted_relation: Lord Willam Dustin rode with Ned to the tower of joy on his great red stallion (dream narration = second-hand recall)

evidence_quote: Ser Arthur Dayne, the Sword of the Morning, had a sad smile on his lips. The hilt of the greatsword Dawn poked up over his right shoulder.
asserted_relation: Ser Arthur Dayne was one of the three Kingsguard who fought at the tower of joy

evidence_quote: Ser Oswell Whent was on one knee, sharpening his blade with a whetstone. Across his white-enameled helm, the black bat of his House spread its wings.
asserted_relation: Ser Oswell Whent was one of the three Kingsguard who fought at the tower of joy

evidence_quote: Between them stood fierce old Ser Gerold Hightower, the White Bull, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard.
asserted_relation: Ser Gerold Hightower was one of the three Kingsguard who fought at the tower of joy

evidence_quote: If he had not gone into Duskendale to rescue Aerys from Lord Darklyn's dungeons, the king might well have died there as Tywin Lannister sacked the town.
asserted_relation: The Defiance of Duskendale took place at Duskendale

evidence_quote: I once brought the queen's father safely out of Duskendale, where he was being held captive by a rebel lord
asserted_relation: Aerys II Targaryen was imprisoned at Duskendale (victim/captive) during the Defiance

evidence_quote: Brought King Aerys II to safety during the Defiance of Duskendale, despite an arrow wound in the chest.
asserted_relation: Barristan Selmy single-handedly rescued Aerys from Duskendale (the signature act)

evidence_quote: Denys Darklyn, Lord of Duskendale, wished to halt this decline. He desired to win a charter for Duskendale … Infuriated at Tywin's refusal, Denys devised a new plan
asserted_relation: Denys Darklyn instigated the Defiance by imprisoning Aerys (the defiant lord, primary agent)

evidence_quote: He had first heard those words from Tywin Lannister outside the walls of Duskendale. He gave me a day to bring out Aerys. Unless I returned with the king by dawn of the following day, he would take the town with steel and fire, he told me.
asserted_relation: Tywin Lannister commanded the siege force outside Duskendale as Hand of the King

evidence_quote: The only exception was young Dontos Hollard, whose life was spared when Ser Barristan Selmy requested it. Aerys, unable to refuse the man who had saved his life, granted this boon.
asserted_relation: Dontos Hollard was a victim/survivor of the post-Defiance executions, spared only by Barristan's plea

evidence_quote: Denys's wife, Lady Serala, had her tongue and womanly parts torn out, after which she was burned alive.
asserted_relation: Serala of Myr was executed in the aftermath of the Defiance

evidence_quote: Ser Jon Hollard, who had been married to Denys's sister, died together with his wife and young son.
asserted_relation: Jon Hollard was executed in the Defiance's aftermath

evidence_quote: Robin Hollard, the squire who had pulled Aerys's beard when he had first been seized, died upon the rack.
asserted_relation: Robin Hollard was tortured to death in the Defiance's aftermath

evidence_quote: the king demanded the deaths of Denys and his immediate family, as well as his uncles, aunts, and distant House Darklyn kinsmen living in Duskendale.
asserted_relation: House Darklyn was attainted and executed collectively in the Defiance's aftermath

evidence_quote: House Hollard, Lord Denys's goodkin, were attainted and executed as well.
asserted_relation: House Hollard was attainted and nearly extinguished in the Defiance's aftermath

evidence_quote: It had been to bring back the Old Way more than for the empty vanity of a crown that Lord Balon had staged his great rebellion. Robert Baratheon had written a bloody end to that hope, with the help of his friend Eddard Stark, but both men were dead now.
asserted_relation: Balon Greyjoy led the ironborn rebellion against the Iron Throne in 289 AC

evidence_quote: Ned had last seen the king nine years before during Balon Greyjoy's rebellion, when the stag and the direwolf had joined to end the pretensions of the self-proclaimed King of the Iron Islands. Since the night they had stood side by side in Greyjoy's fallen stronghold, where Robert had accepted the rebel lord's surrender and Ned had taken his son Theon as hostage and ward, the king had gained at least eight stone.
asserted_relation: Robert Baratheon commanded the loyalist forces that crushed the Greyjoy Rebellion

evidence_quote: Ned had last seen the king nine years before during Balon Greyjoy's rebellion, when the stag and the direwolf had joined to end the pretensions of the self-proclaimed King of the Iron Islands. Since the night they had stood side by side in Greyjoy's fallen stronghold, where Robert had accepted the rebel lord's surrender and Ned had taken his son Theon as hostage and ward, the king had gained at least eight stone.
asserted_relation: Eddard Stark co-commanded loyalist northern forces alongside Robert in the Greyjoy Rebellion

evidence_quote: During her father's first rebellion, Stannis had smashed the Iron Fleet off Fair Isle and subdued Great Wyk in his brother's name.
asserted_relation: Stannis Baratheon commanded the loyalist fleet that destroyed the Iron Fleet at Fair Isle

evidence_quote: During my lord father's rebellion, he sailed into Lannisport with my uncle Euron and burned the Lannister fleet where it lay at anchor. The plan was Euron's, though.
asserted_relation: Victarion Greyjoy led the ironborn naval assault that burned the Lannister fleet at Lannisport

evidence_quote: During my lord father's rebellion, he sailed into Lannisport with my uncle Euron and burned the Lannister fleet where it lay at anchor. The plan was Euron's, though.
asserted_relation: Euron Greyjoy planned and participated in the burning of the Lannister fleet at Lannisport

evidence_quote: Rodrik had been slain by Lord Jason Mallister at Seagard, Maron crushed in the collapse of the old south tower . . . but Stark would have done for them just as quick had the tide of battle chanced to sweep them together.
asserted_relation: Rodrik Greyjoy was killed by Jason Mallister at the storming of Seagard during the rebellion

evidence_quote: Rodrik had been slain by Lord Jason Mallister at Seagard, Maron crushed in the collapse of the old south tower . . . but Stark would have done for them just as quick had the tide of battle chanced to sweep them together.
asserted_relation: Maron Greyjoy was killed in the collapse of the south tower during the storming of Pyke

evidence_quote: The girls giggled over the warrior priest Thoros of Myr, with his flapping red robes and shaven head, until the septa told them that he had once scaled the walls of Pyke with a flaming sword in hand.
asserted_relation: Thoros of Myr was first through the breach at Pyke during the storming, wielding a flaming sword

evidence_quote: Lord Balon Greyjoy rose in rebellion against the Usurper, and Ned Stark called his banners to help his friend Robert. The final battle was on Pyke. When Robert's stonethrowers opened a breach in King Balon's wall, a priest from Myr was the first man through, but I was not far behind. For that I won my knighthood.
asserted_relation: Jorah Mormont fought at the breach of Pyke and was knighted by Robert for his valor

evidence_quote: Lord Jason had slain Rodrik Greyjoy under the walls of the castle, and thrown the ironmen back into the bay.
asserted_relation: Jason Mallister defended Seagard and slew Rodrik Greyjoy during the storming of Seagard

evidence_quote: he served with valor during Balon Greyjoy's Rebellion. King Robert knighted him at Pyke. And yet his name does not appear on your list.
asserted_relation: Jacelyn Bywater fought at Pyke, lost a hand there, and was knighted by Robert for his valor

evidence_quote: Ser Barristan Selmy, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, led the attack on Old Wyk.
asserted_relation: Barristan Selmy led the loyalist attack on Old Wyk during the Greyjoy Rebellion

evidence_quote: The loyalist army broke and ran, with the survivors fleeing back to King's Landing. Robert Baratheon had taken a wound from Rhaegar, and so gave command of the vanguard to Lord Eddard Stark of Winterfell, who chased the loyalists to the capital. There, King Aerys was located in the Red Keep, protected by several thousand loyalists.
asserted_relation: The Sack of King's Landing took place at King's Landing

evidence_quote: We had come late to Robert's cause. It was necessary to demonstrate our loyalty. When I laid those bodies before the throne, no man could doubt that we had forsaken House Targaryen forever.
asserted_relation: Tywin Lannister commanded and ordered the Sack of King's Landing (recalled in-character confession)

evidence_quote: Jaime hauled the last dragonking bodily off the steps, squealing like a pig and smelling like a privy. A single slash across his throat was all it took to end it.
asserted_relation: Jaime Lannister killed King Aerys II during the Sack (the Kingslayer act)

evidence_quote: During the Sack of King's Landing, slew King Aerys II at the foot of the Iron Throne. Thereafter known as the "Kingslayer."
asserted_relation: King Aerys II Targaryen was killed during the Sack of King's Landing

evidence_quote: It was Ser Gregor Clegane who smashed Prince Aegon's head against a wall and raped your sister Elia with his blood and brains still on his hands.
asserted_relation: Gregor Clegane performed the murders of Aegon and Elia during the Sack (recalled second-hand by Tyrion to Oberyn)

evidence_quote: "Elia of Dorne," they all heard Ser Gregor say, when they were close enough to kiss. His deep voice boomed within the helm. "I killed her screaming whelp." He thrust his free hand into Oberyn's unprotected face, pushing steel fingers into his eyes. "Then I raped her." Clegane slammed his fist into the Dornishman's mouth, making splinters of his teeth. "Then I smashed her fucking head in."
asserted_relation: Elia Martell was raped and killed by Gregor Clegane during the Sack — Gregor's own confession

evidence_quote: Ser Amory dragged Princess Rhaenys out from under her father's bed and stabbed her to death.
asserted_relation: Princess Rhaenys (daughter of Rhaegar) was killed during the Sack — recalled by Tyrion to Oberyn

evidence_quote: Ser Amory dragged Princess Rhaenys out from under her father's bed and stabbed her to death. He had some men-at-arms with him, but I do not know their names.
asserted_relation: Amory Lorch killed Princess Rhaenys during the Sack — recalled by Tyrion to Oberyn

evidence_quote: It was Ser Gregor Clegane who smashed Prince Aegon's head against a wall and raped your sister Elia with his blood and brains still on his hands.
asserted_relation: Prince Aegon (son of Rhaegar) was killed during the Sack — recalled by Tyrion to Oberyn

evidence_quote: Beyond the city walls, a hundred pavilions had been raised beside the river, and the common folk came out in the thousands to watch the games.
asserted_relation: The Hand's tourney was held at the tourney grounds outside King's Landing beside the Blackwater Rush

evidence_quote: The Knight of Flowers saluted the king, rode to the far end of the list, and couched his lance, ready. Ser Gregor brought his animal to the line, fighting with the reins. And suddenly it began. The Mountain's stallion broke in a hard gallop, plunging forward wildly, while the mare charged as smooth as a flow of silk. Ser Gregor wrenched his shield into position, juggled with his lance, and all the while fought to hold his unruly mount on a straight line, and suddenly Loras Tyrell was on him, placing the point of his lance just there, and in an eye blink the Mountain was falling.
asserted_relation: Loras Tyrell jousted in the Hand's tourney, unhorsing Gregor Clegane; Sandor Clegane yielded the championship to him

evidence_quote: Gregor Clegane killed the horse with a single blow of such ferocity that it half severed the animal's neck. Cheers turned to shrieks in a heartbeat. The stallion went to its knees, screaming as it died. By then Gregor was striding down the lists toward Ser Loras Tyrell, his bloody sword clutched in his fist.
asserted_relation: Gregor Clegane jousted in the Hand's tourney; was unhorsed by Loras, then attacked Loras with a sword before being stopped by Sandor

evidence_quote: But as Gregor lifted his sword for the killing blow, a rasping voice warned, "Leave him be," and a steel-clad hand wrenched him away from the boy.
asserted_relation: Sandor Clegane jousted in the Hand's tourney (eliminated Jaime Lannister), saved Loras from Gregor, and was awarded the championship

evidence_quote: The Hound just managed to stay in his saddle. He jerked his mount around hard and rode back to the lists for the second pass. Jaime Lannister tossed down his broken lance and snatched up a fresh one, jesting with his squire. The Hound spurred forward at a hard gallop. Lannister rode to meet him. This time, when Jaime shifted his seat, Sandor Clegane shifted with him. Both lances exploded, and by the time the splinters had settled, a riderless blood bay was trotting off in search of grass while Ser Jaime Lannister rolled in the dirt, golden and dented.
asserted_relation: Jaime Lannister jousted in the Hand's tourney; reached the final four but was eliminated by Sandor Clegane

evidence_quote: Jory, Alyn, and Harwin rode for Winterfell and the north. "Jory looks a beggar among these others," Septa Mordane sniffed when he appeared. Sansa could only agree. Jory's armor was blue-grey plate without device or ornament, and a thin grey cloak hung from his shoulders like a soiled rag. Yet he acquitted himself well, unhorsing Horas Redwyne in his first joust and one of the Freys in his second.
asserted_relation: Jory Cassel jousted in the Hand's tourney for Winterfell and the north

evidence_quote: Later a hedge knight in a checkered cloak disgraced himself by killing Beric Dondarrion's horse, and was declared forfeit. Lord Beric shifted his saddle to a new mount, only to be knocked right off it by Thoros of Myr.
asserted_relation: Beric Dondarrion jousted in the Hand's tourney and was eliminated by Thoros of Myr

evidence_quote: The victor was the red priest, Thoros of Myr, a madman who shaved his head and fought with a flaming sword. He had won melees before; the fire sword frightened the mounts of the other riders, and nothing frightened Thoros.
asserted_relation: Thoros of Myr won the melee at the Hand's tourney with his flaming sword

evidence_quote: That afternoon a boy named Anguy, an unheralded commoner from the Dornish Marches, won the archery competition, outshooting Ser Balon Swann and Jalabhar Xho at a hundred paces after all the other bowmen had been eliminated at the shorter distances.
asserted_relation: Anguy won the archery competition at the Hand's tourney, defeating Balon Swann and Jalabhar Xho

evidence_quote: Ser Balon Swann also fell to Gregor, and Lord Renly to the Hound.
asserted_relation: Balon Swann jousted in the Hand's tourney (unhorsed by Gregor Clegane) and competed in the archery

evidence_quote: The Kingslayer rode brilliantly. He overthrew Ser Andar Royce and the Marcher Lord Bryce Caron as easily as if he were riding at rings, and then took a hard-fought match from white-haired Barristan Selmy, who had won his first two tilts against men thirty and forty years his junior.
asserted_relation: Barristan Selmy jousted in the Hand's tourney; won his first two tilts before being eliminated by Jaime Lannister

evidence_quote: That afternoon a boy named Anguy, an unheralded commoner from the Dornish Marches, won the archery competition, outshooting Ser Balon Swann and Jalabhar Xho at a hundred paces after all the other bowmen had been eliminated at the shorter distances.
asserted_relation: Jalabhar Xho competed in the archery at the Hand's tourney; was runner-up to Anguy

evidence_quote: Ser Balon Swann also fell to Gregor, and Lord Renly to the Hound. Renly was unhorsed so violently that he seemed to fly backward off his charger, legs in the air.
asserted_relation: Renly Baratheon jousted in the Hand's tourney; was unhorsed by Sandor Clegane

evidence_quote: Alyn and Harwin fared less well; Harwin was unhorsed in his first tilt by Ser Meryn of the Kingsguard, while Alyn fell to Ser Balon Swann.
asserted_relation: Meryn Trant jousted in the Hand's tourney as part of the Kingsguard contingent

evidence_quote: Ser Aron Santagar and Lothor Brune tilted thrice without result; Ser Aron fell afterward to Lord Jason Mallister, and Brune to Yohn Royce's younger son, Robar.
asserted_relation: Yohn Royce competed in the Hand's tourney (his sons Andar and Robar also jousted)

evidence_quote: The Kingslayer rode brilliantly. He overthrew Ser Andar Royce and the Marcher Lord Bryce Caron as easily as if he were riding at rings
asserted_relation: Andar Royce jousted in the Hand's tourney; was eliminated by Jaime Lannister

evidence_quote: His last match of the day was against the younger Royce. Ser Robar's ancestral runes proved small protection as Ser Loras split his shield and drove him from his saddle to crash with an awful clangor in the dirt.
asserted_relation: Robar Royce jousted in the Hand's tourney; was eliminated by Loras Tyrell in the semifinal

evidence_quote: The Kingslayer rode brilliantly. He overthrew Ser Andar Royce and the Marcher Lord Bryce Caron as easily as if he were riding at rings
asserted_relation: Bryce Caron jousted in the Hand's tourney; was eliminated by Jaime Lannister

evidence_quote: Ser Aron Santagar and Lothor Brune tilted thrice without result; Ser Aron fell afterward to Lord Jason Mallister, and Brune to Yohn Royce's younger son, Robar.
asserted_relation: Jason Mallister jousted in the Hand's tourney

evidence_quote: Ser Aron Santagar and Lothor Brune tilted thrice without result; Ser Aron fell afterward to Lord Jason Mallister
asserted_relation: Aron Santagar jousted in the Hand's tourney; eliminated by Jason Mallister after three tied passes

evidence_quote: In his third match, he rode three passes at a freerider named Lothor Brune whose armor was as drab as his own. Neither man lost his seat, but Brune's lance was steadier and his blows better placed, and the king gave him the victory.
asserted_relation: Lothor Brune jousted as a freerider in the Hand's tourney; defeated Jory Cassel on judge's decision

evidence_quote: Yet he acquitted himself well, unhorsing Horas Redwyne in his first joust and one of the Freys in his second.
asserted_relation: Horas Redwyne jousted in the Hand's tourney; was eliminated by Jory Cassel

evidence_quote: The twins Ser Horas and Ser Hobber, whose shields displayed the grape cluster sigil of the Redwynes, burgundy on blue.
asserted_relation: Hobber Redwyne jousted in the Hand's tourney alongside his twin Horas

evidence_quote: Patrek Mallister, Lord Jason's son.
asserted_relation: Patrek Mallister rode in the Hand's tourney

evidence_quote: Jory, Alyn, and Harwin rode for Winterfell and the north. "Jory looks a beggar among these others," Septa Mordane sniffed when he appeared. Sansa could only agree. Jory's armor was blue-grey plate without device or ornament, and a thin grey cloak hung from his shoulders like a soiled rag. Yet he acquitted himself well, unhorsing Horas Redwyne in his first joust and one of the Freys in his second. In his third match, he rode three passes at a freerider named Lothor Brune whose armor was as drab as his own. Neither man lost his seat, but Brune's lance was steadier and his blows better placed, and the king gave him the victory. Alyn and Harwin fared less well; Harwin was unhorsed in his first tilt by Ser Meryn of the Kingsguard, while Alyn fell to Ser Balon Swann.
asserted_relation: Alyn jousted for Winterfell and the north in the Hand's tourney; was eliminated by Balon Swann

evidence_quote: Alyn and Harwin fared less well; Harwin was unhorsed in his first tilt by Ser Meryn of the Kingsguard, while Alyn fell to Ser Balon Swann.
asserted_relation: Harwin jousted for Winterfell and the north in the Hand's tourney; was eliminated by Meryn Trant

evidence_quote: the silent sisters had dressed him in his best velvet tunic, with a high collar to cover the ruin the lance had made of his throat
asserted_relation: Hugh of the Vale (Jon Arryn's former squire, newly knighted) jousted in the Hand's tourney and was killed by Gregor Clegane's lance through his throat

evidence_quote: Sansa and Septa Mordane were given places of high honor, to the left of the raised dais where the king himself sat beside his queen.
asserted_relation: Robert Baratheon attended and presided over the Hand's tourney from the royal dais

evidence_quote: Sansa and Septa Mordane were given places of high honor, to the left of the raised dais where the king himself sat beside his queen.
asserted_relation: Cersei Lannister attended the Hand's tourney feast and jousting (later departed in anger after quarrel with Robert)

evidence_quote: Ned walked with the king to the jousting field. He had promised to watch the final tilts with Sansa; Septa Mordane was ill today, and his daughter was determined not to miss the end of the jousting. As he saw Robert to his place, he noted that Cersei Lannister had chosen not to appear; the place beside the king was empty.
asserted_relation: Eddard Stark attended the Hand's tourney, which was held in his honor

evidence_quote: Sansa rode to the Hand's tourney with Septa Mordane and Jeyne Poole, in a litter with curtains of yellow silk so fine she could see right through them.
asserted_relation: Sansa Stark attended the Hand's tourney as a spectator in the high lords' gallery

evidence_quote: When Prince Joffrey seated himself to her right, she felt her throat tighten.
asserted_relation: Joffrey Baratheon attended the Hand's tourney feast and final jousts

evidence_quote: "Your mother was my queen of beauty once," the man said quietly. His breath smelled of mint. "You have her hair." His fingers brushed against her cheek as he stroked one auburn lock. Quite abruptly he turned and walked away.
asserted_relation: Petyr Baelish attended the Hand's tourney and approached Sansa in the gallery

evidence_quote: the tourney staged by Lord Whent at Harrenhal beside the Gods Eye
asserted_relation: Tourney at Harrenhal was located at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: Yet when the jousting began, the day belonged to Rhaegar Targaryen. The crown prince wore the armor he would die in: gleaming black plate with the three-headed dragon of his House wrought in rubies on the breast. A plume of scarlet silk streamed behind him when he rode, and it seemed no lance could touch him. Brandon fell to him, and Bronze Yohn Royce, and even the splendid Ser Arthur Dayne, the Sword of the Morning.
asserted_relation: Rhaegar Targaryen was champion of the joust at the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: Robert had been jesting with Jon and old Lord Hunter as the prince circled the field after unhorsing Ser Barristan in the final tilt to claim the champion's crown.
asserted_relation: Barristan Selmy jousted at the tourney at Harrenhal, falling to Rhaegar in the final tilt

evidence_quote: Brandon fell to him, and Bronze Yohn Royce, and even the splendid Ser Arthur Dayne, the Sword of the Morning.
asserted_relation: Arthur Dayne jousted at the tourney at Harrenhal, falling to Rhaegar

evidence_quote: Brandon fell to him, and Bronze Yohn Royce, and even the splendid Ser Arthur Dayne, the Sword of the Morning.
asserted_relation: Brandon Stark jousted at the tourney at Harrenhal, falling to Rhaegar

evidence_quote: Brandon fell to him, and Bronze Yohn Royce, and even the splendid Ser Arthur Dayne, the Sword of the Morning.
asserted_relation: Yohn Royce jousted at the tourney at Harrenhal, falling to Rhaegar

evidence_quote: He remembered Brandon's laughter, and Robert's berserk valor in the melee, the way he laughed as he unhorsed men left and right.
asserted_relation: Robert Baratheon competed in the melee at the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: Whoever he was, the old gods gave strength to his arm. The porcupine knight fell first, then the pitchfork knight, and lastly the knight of the two towers. None were well loved, so the common folk cheered lustily for the Knight of the Laughing Tree, as the new champion soon was called.
asserted_relation: The Knight of the Laughing Tree jousted at the tourney at Harrenhal, defeating three knights

evidence_quote: Afterward, Ser Os well Whent helped Jaime to his feet, and the White Bull himself, Lord Commander Ser Gerold Hightower, fastened the snowy cloak of the Kingsguard about his shoulders.
asserted_relation: Oswell Whent was present and jousted at the tourney at Harrenhal (wiki: defended Walter's daughter's honor in the joust)

evidence_quote: The king himself was there, with his son the dragon prince. The White Swords had come, to welcome a new brother to their ranks.
asserted_relation: Aerys II Targaryen attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: It was the year of false spring, and he was eighteen again, down from the Eyrie to the tourney at Harrenhal.
asserted_relation: Eddard Stark attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: Prince Rhaegar Targaryen urged his horse past his own wife, the Dornish princess Elia Martell, to lay the queen of beauty's laurel in Lyanna's lap. He could see it still: a crown of winter roses, blue as frost.
asserted_relation: Lyanna Stark attended the tourney at Harrenhal and was crowned queen of love and beauty by Rhaegar

evidence_quote: Prince Rhaegar Targaryen urged his horse past his own wife, the Dornish princess Elia Martell, to lay the queen of beauty's laurel in Lyanna's lap.
asserted_relation: Elia Martell attended the tourney at Harrenhal as Rhaegar's wife

evidence_quote: Robert had been jesting with Jon and old Lord Hunter as the prince circled the field after unhorsing Ser Barristan in the final tilt to claim the champion's crown.
asserted_relation: Jon Arryn attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: Robert had been jesting with Jon and old Lord Hunter as the prince circled the field after unhorsing Ser Barristan in the final tilt to claim the champion's crown.
asserted_relation: Eon Hunter attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: He remembered Jaime Lannister, a golden youth in scaled white armor, kneeling on the grass in front of the king's pavilion and making his vows to protect and defend King Aerys.
asserted_relation: Jaime Lannister attended the tourney at Harrenhal where he was inducted into the Kingsguard (left early by Aerys's command)

evidence_quote: the White Bull himself, Lord Commander Ser Gerold Hightower, fastened the snowy cloak of the Kingsguard about his shoulders. All six White Swords were there to welcome their newest brother.
asserted_relation: Gerold Hightower attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: Under Harren's roof he ate and drank with the wolves, and many of their sworn swords besides, barrowdown men and moose and bears and mermen. The dragon prince sang a song so sad it made the wolf maid sniffle, but when her pup brother teased her for crying she poured wine over his head.
asserted_relation: Howland Reed (the crannogman) attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: The dragon prince sang a song so sad it made the wolf maid sniffle, but when her pup brother teased her for crying she poured wine over his head.
asserted_relation: Benjen Stark (the pup) attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: The crannogman saw a maid with laughing purple eyes dance with a white sword, a red snake, and the lord of griffins, and lastly with the quiet wolf . . . but only after the wild wolf spoke to her on behalf of a brother too shy to leave his bench.
asserted_relation: Ashara Dayne attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: The crannogman saw a maid with laughing purple eyes dance with a white sword, a red snake, and the lord of griffins, and lastly with the quiet wolf . . .
asserted_relation: Oberyn Martell (the red snake) attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: The crannogman saw a maid with laughing purple eyes dance with a white sword, a red snake, and the lord of griffins, and lastly with the quiet wolf . . .
asserted_relation: Jon Connington (lord of griffins) attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: The storm lord was on hand, and the rose lord as well.
asserted_relation: Mace Tyrell (the rose lord) attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: The storm lord drank down the knight of skulls and kisses in a wine-cup war.
asserted_relation: Richard Lonmouth (the knight of skulls and kisses) attended the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: The tourney was announced in 280 AC by Lord Walter Whent
asserted_relation: Walter Whent hosted the tourney at Harrenhal

evidence_quote: a fire at Summerhall, a pleasure castle of House Targaryen in the Dornish Marches
asserted_relation: The tragedy occurred at Summerhall castle

evidence_quote: Treason and turmoil followed, as night follows day, ending at Summerhall in sorcery, fire, and grief.
asserted_relation: Aegon V Targaryen died in the Tragedy at Summerhall (recalled second-hand by Barristan)

evidence_quote: The cause of the fire is as yet unknown, but was connected with King Aegon's desire to restore dragons to the Seven Kingdoms.
asserted_relation: Aegon V ordered the dragon-egg hatching attempt that caused the fire — he is the initiating agent

evidence_quote: The conflagration caused the deaths of, among others, King Aegon V Targaryen, his eldest son, Prince Duncan Targaryen, and Ser Duncan the Tall, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard.
asserted_relation: Prince Duncan Targaryen died in the Tragedy at Summerhall

evidence_quote: The conflagration caused the deaths of, among others, King Aegon V Targaryen, his eldest son, Prince Duncan Targaryen, and Ser Duncan the Tall, the Lord Commander of the Kingsguard.
asserted_relation: Ser Duncan the Tall, Lord Commander of the Kingsguard, died in the Tragedy at Summerhall
